data.title: RICS: UK House Price Balance Falls in February
data.description:
The UK Royal Institution of Chartered Surveyors house price balance decreased to 11% in February, from the revised 21% in the previous month, according to data released Thursday.

Analysts expected a 20% reading for the month.

Most respondents see house prices rising over the next twelve months amid expectations of reduced momentum in the short term.

data.title: Mapletree Logistics Trust to Divest Warehouse for SG$8 Million
data.description:
Mapletree Logistics Trust agreed to dispose of its 31 Penjuru Lane property in Singapore for SG$7.8 million, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Wednesday.

JTC Corporation has been granted in-principle approval for the transaction.

The divestment is part of the manager's efforts to rejuvenate the portfolio through divestment of assets that are no longer part of the group's long-term strategy.

The property comprises a three-story warehouse and an ancillary office. The property's remaining land lease is of around seven years.

Following the divestment, the trust's portfolio will consist of 176 properties.

Shares of the trust were up nearly 2% in recent trading.

data.title: Aether Catalyst Solutions, Inc. Announces a Non-Brokered Private Placement
data.description:
Burnaby, British Columbia--(Newsfile Corp. - March 13, 2025) - Aether Catalyst Solutions, Inc. (FSE: 2QZ) ("ATHR" or the "Company") announces that it proposes to raise up to $75,000 through a non-brokered private placement of up to 1,875,000 units at a price of $0.04 per unit. Each unit will consist of one common share and one-half of a transferable common share purchase warrant of the Company, with each whole warrant exercisable for a period of 12 months from the date of closing at a price of $0.10 per share.

Finder's fees or commissions may be payable to certain eligible persons. All securities issued in connection with the private placement are subject to a 4-month hold period in Canada.

The terms of the financing are subject to applicable securities laws and regulatory approval. Additional details regarding the Offering can be found in the Form 9 filed in accordance with the rules of the Canadian Securities Exchange, a copy of which is accessible at www.thecse.com under the Company's profile.

The gross proceeds of the private placement will be used for general working capital purposes.

The Company confirms that there is no material fact or material change about the Company that has not already been generally disclosed. Closing is expected to occur March 19, 2025.

ABOUT THE COMPANY:

Aether Catalyst Solutions, Inc. is focused on providing an order of magnitude cost reduction in automotive catalytic converter catalyst, while meeting, or exceeding government emission standards. Aether is working to quickly advance its technology through rapid screening of new materials directed at enhancing end of life conversion levels after accelerated aging. While Aether's primary focus has been automotive applications, the company is also developing catalysts to address small motors emissions - a significant contributor to urban air pollution.

FOR FURTHER INFORMATION PLEASE CONTACT:

Aether Catalyst Solutions, Inc.

Paul Woodward

President

Tel: 604 690-3797

http://www.aethercatalyst.com

The Canadian Securities Exchange ("CSE") or any other securities regulatory authority has not reviewed and does not accept responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this management prepared news release.

NOT FOR DISTRIBUTION TO U.S. NEWSWIRE SERVICES OR FOR

DISSEMINATION IN THE UNITED STATES

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/244428

data.title: Jet2 Sets Final Repurchase Price for GBP302 Million Bond Buyback
data.description:
Jet2 on Wednesday announced the final repurchase price for 387.4 million pounds sterling of 1.625% guaranteed senior unsecured convertible bonds due 2026.

The leisure travel company will pay 103,577.29 pounds per 100,000 in principal amount of bonds accepted for purchase, which amounted to 301.5 million pounds.

Settlement is expected on March 18.

data.title: Global Forex and Fixed Income Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering FX and Fixed Income. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

The cooler February U.S. inflation print provides some relief, but the test of tariffs' impact on prices lies in the months ahead, UOB says. U.S. tariff uncertainty and other policies keep risk for inflation elevated, says senior economist Alvin Liew in a note. While goods inflation appeared tame in February, it is expected to push higher as President Trump's tariffs on China and other major trading partners as well as on key products start to hit home, he says. While the downside risk to growth has increased, Trump policy uncertainty would continue to argue the case for the Fed to stay on hold for longer, Liew says. UOB expects the Fed to deliver a 25bp cut in 2Q and then hold rates for the rest of the year. (monica.gupta@wsj.com)

data.title: China, Hong Kong shares weighed down by tech stocks
data.description:
China and Hong Kong shares fell on Thursday, pressured by tech and AI-related firms, although gains in banking and energy stocks helped cushion the broader decline.

** At the midday break, the Shanghai Composite index was down 0.4% and China's blue-chip CSI300 index fell 0.2%.

** The Hang Seng Index weakened 0.7% in Hong Kong, while the Hang Seng Tech Index slipped 2.2%.

** Tech shares led the losses in both onshore and offshore trading. The CSI AI Index dropped 2.2%, heading for its biggest single-day decline in two weeks, while Alibaba and Tencent (700.HK) retreated 2.7% and 1.4%, respectively, in Hong Kong.

** However, the banking index (.CSI399986) climbed 0.6% and energy shares rallied 3.2%, helping limit declines.

** On Wednesday, Donald Trump threatened to escalate a global trade war with further tariffs on European Union goods, as major U.S. trading partners said they would retaliate for trade barriers already erected by the U.S. president.

** The resilience of Chinese stocks in the face of the U.S. equity selloff could be unsustainable as the economies could deteriorate as U.S. demand – the sole pillar of global growth in the past two years – vanishes and tariffs bite, analysts at BCA Research said in a note.

** Around the region, MSCI's Asia ex-Japan stock index (.MIAPJ0000PUS) fell 0.49% while Japan's Nikkei index was up 0.42%.

data.title: Nebraska governor signs bill to regulate crypto ATMs, citing growing fraud
data.description:
The governor of Nebraska, Jim Pillen, has signed legislation to protect against cryptocurrency fraud as crypto ATM crime skyrockets in the United States.

“Cryptocurrency is an important, emerging industry, and we’ve been working hard to build Nebraska into a cryptocurrency leader,” said Governor Pillen on March 12 following the signing of the bill. 

“An important part of these efforts is to make sure that we have guardrails to prevent criminals from taking advantage of Nebraskans,” he added.

The bipartisan legislation establishes the “Controllable Electronic Record Fraud Prevention Act,” which is designed to help combat fraud and protect users of crypto kiosks and ATMs.

According to the Federal Trade Commission, victims have lost over $65 million to crypto ATM fraud in the first half of 2024. “Fraud losses at BTMs (Bitcoin ATMs) are skyrocketing, increasing nearly tenfold from 2020 to 2023,” the commission reported in September. 

The bill, LB 609, was introduced on Jan. 22 by Senator Eliot Bostar. It stipulates that crypto ATM and kiosk operators must be licensed under Nebraska’s Money Transmitters Act and registered and approved by the Department of Banking and Finance. Operators must provide quarterly reports on kiosk locations, names and transaction data.

It also implements transaction limits of $2,000 per day for new users and $5,000 per day for existing customers, and fees cannot exceed 18% of the transaction value. 

New customers who report fraud within 90 days can receive a full refund including fees, while existing customers can be refunded for the fees associated with fraudulent transactions.

Kiosk operators must also display fraud warnings and appoint a compliance officer to enforce fraud prevention measures, it states. 

The US crypto ATM network shrunk by more than 1,200 machines earlier this month after Illinois Senator Dick Durbin introduced similar legislation. 

“Nebraska is open for business in the cryptocurrency space,” commented state Department of Banking director Kelly Lammers, who added, “those that target our citizens … using crypto ATMs as part of their transfer method, we will soon have a team that will be watching even more closely.”

While being supportive of crypto, Nebraska has yet to join the 21 US states that have proposed legislation to establish strategic crypto reserves, according to the Bitcoin Reserve Monitor.

data.title: Anglo American Unit Prices Redemption of Notes
data.description:
Anglo American (, AGL.JO) subsidiary Anglo American Capital priced its offer to repurchase multiple series of US dollar- and euro-denominated notes.

Based on the results of the offer launched on Feb. 26, the mining company's unit will redeem $394 million of 4% senior notes due September 2027, $110.7 million of 4.750% senior notes due April 2027, $379.9 million of 2.250% senior notes due March 2028, and 126 million euros of 4.5% guaranteed notes due September 2028 at a per-note price of $987.25, $1,002.96, $936.36 and 1,052.24 euros, respectively.

Anglo American Capital indicated that it has sole discretion to increase the maximum acceptance amount for the redemption of the notes. Early settlement is expected by Friday.

data.title: Zydus Lifesciences' Venture Capital Arm Investing in Illexcor Therapeutics
data.description:
Zynext Ventures USA, the venture capital arm of Zydus Lifesciences , has agreed to invest of an undisclosed amount in biopharmaceutical company Illexcor Therapeutics.

The investment is aimed at advancing novel oral therapy for sickle cell disease, according to a Wednesday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

Illexcor is developing oral therapies for sickle cell disease. The lead asset, currently in preclinical development, is designed to bind to Hemoglobin S and effectively block polymerization and sickling, offering the potential for disease-modifying clinical benefits.

Zydus Lifesciences shares were up nearly 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Urban Logistics REIT Says Majority of Shareholders Support Shift to Internalized Management
data.description:
Urban Logistics REIT said Wednesday that a significant majority of shareholders it spoke with expressed support for the company's plan to internalize its management structure.

The real estate investment trust unveiled its plans on March 7 and launched a comprehensive dialogue with shareholders afterward. It said the talks continue.

Meanwhile, Urban Logistics also clarified that it did not receive any approach from a potential suitor.

data.title: Major Japan union group says agreements struck for average 5.37% pay hike
data.description:
A major Japanese labour union group said on Thursday its member unions had struck agreements for hefty wage hikes with employers for a third consecutive year.

UA Zensen, an umbrella group representing retail, restaurant and other industry unions, said its members had received an average 5.37% increase in monthly wages for full-time workers. Part-timers gained a 6.53% hike.

The results compared with last year's average 5.91% increase for full-time workers and 6.45% for part-timers.

The group had set a target of achieving a 6% increase for full-time workers and 7% for part-timers this year.

UA Zensen unions have 1.9 million workers at 2,200 companies including retail conglomerate Aeon , Takeda Pharmaceutical and Toray Industries .

Japanese policymakers and employers have been in agreement that broad-based pay hikes are needed to help workers cope with sharply higher inflation and to retain staff amid labour shortages.

On Wednesday, a swathe of Japan's biggest companies concluded annual labour negotiations with many meeting union demands in full. Electronics conglomerate Hitachi , for example, said it had agreed to a record 6.2% increase in monthly wages.

Economists expect Japan Inc's average pay hike for 2025 to be similar to last year's 5.1% rise, which marked the sharpest increase in 33 years and enabled the central bank to exit its decade-long super-loose monetary policy.

Rengo, Japan's largest labour union umbrella group with 7 million members, will release a preliminary report on agreed terms on March 14. Its unions were seeking an average hike of 6.09%, up from 5.85% last year.

data.title: Turkey - Factors To Watch on March 13
data.description:
Here are news, reports and events that may affect Turkish financial markets on Thursday.

The lira was quoted at 36.5900-36.6050 against the U.S. dollar at 0420 GMT, after closing at 36.5965 on Wednesday.

The main BIST 100 share index closed 1.36% higher at 10,580.26 points on Wednesday.

GLOBAL MARKETS

Tech stocks led advances in Asia on Thursday, taking their cue from Wall Street's gains after tepid inflation data allayed concerns over the state of the U.S. economy.

ERDOGAN SCHEDULE

President Tayyip Erdogan will attend an awards ceremony in Ankara (1100 GMT), and then will have a fast-breaking dinner with MPs in Parliament (1600 GMT).

MINISTERS IN UKRAINE

Trade Minister Omer Bolat and Agriculture Minister Ibrahim Yumakli will meet Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskiy in Kyiv.

PARLIAMENT

Parliament's Planning and Budget Commission will debate a legislation to increase bayram (eid) bonus payments to pensioners (0700 GMT).

CENTRAL BANK MINUTES (TRINT=ECI)

The central bank will announce the minutes of last week's monetary policy committee meeting (1100 GMT). It will also announce its weekly data, including on forex reserves (1130 GMT).

HOUSE SALES

The Turkish Statistical Institute will announce house sales data for February (0700 GMT).

POLISH PM

Poland's Prime Minister Donald Tusk said he urged Turkey to play a key role in any peace process for Ukraine during talks with President Tayyip Erdogan in Ankara on Wednesday.

TURKEY SHALE FIELDS

Turkish national oil company TPAO has signed a joint venture agreement with U.S. oil producer Continental Resources to develop shale fields in the country’s Diyarbakir Basin, Turkish Energy Minister Alparslan Bayraktar said on social media platform X on Wednesday.

data.title: Verity Resources Plans AU$1.5 Million Share Placement; Shares Hit 52-Week Low
data.description:
Verity Resources is seeking AU$1.5 million through a share placement, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

The company received firm commitments to raise AU$307,500 by issuing 25.6 million ordinary fully paid shares at AU$0.012 each.

Participants are entitled to receive one free attaching unlisted option for every two shares subscribed for, exercisable at AU$0.022 per share and expiring on March 19, 2028.

The company also plans to raise an additional AU$1.2 million via a placement of 99.4 million shares and 49.7 million options under the same terms. This is subject to shareholder approval at a general meeting.

Funds raised will be used to advance the Monument gold project in Western Australia.

Verity Resources' shares were down 13% in recent Thursday trade, earlier hitting a 52-week low.

data.title: Wharf Holdings: To Maintain Prudent Financial Managment Amid Headwinds >0004.HK
Wall Street Journal - March 13
data.description:
The following are the top stories in the Wall Street Journal. Reuters has not verified these stories and does not vouch for their accuracy.

- Intel named Lip-Bu Tan as its new chief executive, selecting a former board member and longtime industry executive to help turn around the beleaguered semiconductor company.

- Sunnova Energy , one of United States' largest residential solar companies, is preparing to engage with creditors to explore options, including a potential bankruptcy filing, over its roughly $8.5 billion in debt.

- Algoma Steel Group expects tariffs to hurt its finances and operations, though the company said it is too early to estimate the full extent of the effects.

- Chinese authorities summoned Walmart officials for a dressing-down this week after receiving complaints that the retailer was pressuring some Chinese suppliers to cut prices to absorb the cost of U.S. tariffs.

- Fossil Group plans to close 50 stores and cut jobs in a turnaround plan to focus on its core Fossil brand platform in a cost savings effort.

- Alimentation Couche-Tard will continue its friendly pursuit of Seven & i , executives said, sounding confident that they can overcome antitrust issues that could block the prospective acquisition in the U.S.

- A U.S. federal judge blocked most of a White House executive order punishing Perkins Coie, saying the administration had put itself at odds with the First Amendment by targeting the law firm based on President Donald Trump's dislike of its work for his political opponents.

data.title: Wharf Holdings Declares Second Interim Dividend of HK$0.20 >0004.HK
India 10-year bond yield glued to key level despite easing inflation
data.description:
Indian government bond yields were largely unchanged on Thursday, with the benchmark yield hovering around a key level as upbeat sentiment from easing inflation data was blunted by worries over escalations in the global tariff war.

The benchmark 10-year yield (IN067934G=CC) was at 6.6835% as of 10:00 a.m. IST, compared with its previous close of 6.6821%.

"Despite positive developments, traders have been unable to take the benchmark yield below the key technical level of 6.68%. We have seen the yield reversing from this point multiple times over the last one month and that seems to be playing out again," a trader with a primary dealership said.

U.S. yields rose on Wednesday on concerns about the potential inflationary impact of the global trade war, which outweighed optimism over slowing retail inflation in February.

The U.S. consumer price index rose 0.2% last month and 2.8% in the 12 months through February. Economists had forecast the CPI gaining 0.3% month-on-month and advancing 2.9% year-on-year, per a Reuters poll.

Indian bond yields eased slightly on Wednesday after domestic retail inflation eased to 3.61% last month, the lowest since July, and was also down from 4.26% in January. A Reuters poll had pegged the reading at 3.98%.

The lower reading has boosted the odds of a rate cut by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) for the second straight meeting in April.

Most market participants now expect at least 50 basis points of further reductions, with a 25-bp cut anticipated in April. Barclays said it does not rule out the possibility of a larger-than-usual easing of 35 bps.

Meanwhile, traders believe a big uptick in bond yields remains capped as the RBI bought bonds worth 500 billion rupees ($5.74 billion) on Wednesday and is set to purchase a similar quantum of debt next week.

($1 = 87.0670 Indian rupees)

data.title: India stocks, rupee, swaps, call at 10:00 a.m. IST
data.description:
STOCKS:

The benchmark BSE Sensex was up 0.3% to 74,270, while the broader NSE Nifty 50 rose 0.3% to 22,531, tracking global market trends, and as investors drew comfort from softer-than-expected inflation data in the U.S. and India.

RUPEE:

The Indian rupee rose nearly 0.2% versus the U.S. dollar to 87.0625, in trading thinned out by a technical outage while modest gains in Asian currencies aided an upward bias.

GOVERNMENT BONDS:

The benchmark 10-year bond (IN067934G=CC) was quoted at 100.73 rupees, with the yield nearly unchanged at 6.6835%, as positive cues from lower inflation readings were offset by tariff worries.

OVERNIGHT INDEX SWAPS:

The one-year overnight index swap rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) was down nearly 2 basis points at 6.105%, while the five-year swap rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) declined a little over 1 bp to 5.955%.

CALL MONEY:

India's overnight call money rate (INROND=) was at 6.40%, and the overnight TREPS rate (INTREPTOT=TCCL) was at 6.30%.

data.title: Metallurgical of China Taps VP as Acting CFO
data.description:
Metallurgical of China appointed Bai Xiaohu, executive director and vice president, as acting chief financial officer.

The appointment followed former chief financial officer Zou Hongying's resignation as vice president and chief accountant, according to a Wednesday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

The construction engineering company will complete the selection of the new CFO as soon as possible.

data.title: Market Chatter: HSBC Shifts Some Bankers to Temporary Retention in Investment Banking Overhaul
data.description:
HSBC Holdings is placing certain bankers on short-term retainers as it winds down selective investment banking units under a wider overhaul, Bloomberg News reported Wednesday, citing people familiar with the matter.

The British bank placed bankers on work arrangements ranging from three to six months to finish client mandates in certain cases. The news outlet reported that it is unclear how many bankers are impacted.

HSBC did not immediately respond to MT Newswires' request for comment.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: India's Sensex Up 0.3%, Led by Industrial, Financial Stocks — Market Talk
data.description: Indian shares are higher as concerns about the country's inflation ease following slower-than-expected consumer-price growth data for February. Industrial and financial stocks are leading the gains. Tata Steel is up 1.4% and Bajaj Finserv is 0.9% higher. Among individual movers, Bharat Electronics is up 2.1% after it wins an INR24.63 billion defense contract to supply radars. Investors are focusing on India's trade data due later in the day as well as any developments in U.S. trade and foreign policies. The benchmark Sensex is up 0.3% at 74227.88. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

data.title: Boustead Singapore to Dispose Of Certain Businesses to UI Holdings
data.description:
Boustead Singapore through its subsidiaries, BP Unity and BP-Real Estate Investments, agreed to dispose of certain fund and property management businesses of the group to UI Holdings, according to a late Wednesday filing.

As a consideration, UI Holdings will issue 35,505,812 new shares worth $78.6 million to Boustead Singapore, resulting in Boustead Singapore acquiring a 24.1% stake in UH Holdings.

Under the agreement, Boustead Singapore will also sell its 50% stake in Echo Base-BP Capital (Vietnam) to UI Holdings, a private real estate investor and fund manager.

In addition, the two parties will also discuss the potential sale of 3% of KTG & Boustead Industrial Logistics to UI Holdings for $2.3 million.

Furthermore, a Boustead Singapore unit will make a capital commitment of at least $11.6 million in UH Holdings.

data.title: Beng Kuang Marine Issues 3,000 New Shares Following Warrant Exercise
data.description:
Beng Kuang Marine allotted and issued 3,000 new shares pursuant to the exercise of warrants at a price of SG$0.22 per share, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Wednesday.

Following the issue of these shares, the number of issued shares of the company increased to 199,213,406 shares.

Shares of the company were up over 2% in recent trading.

data.title: Wharf Holdings 2024 Rev HK$12.115B Vs. HK$18.95B >0004.HK
Wharf Holdings 2024 Loss HK$3.22B Vs. Net HK$945.0M >0004.HK
Yuan firms on rising China bond yields; trade war in focus
data.description:
China's yuan firmed against the dollar on Thursday, underpinned by a rise in domestic bond yields as investors assessed the impact of an escalating global trade war.

Contrary to market consensus, the yuan has held steady despite higher tariffs, while government bond yields have risen rather than fallen, said Larry Hu, an economist at Macquarie.

Hu attributed recent weakness in the dollar and the success of local startup DeepSeek as reasons behind improved risk sentiment, which in turn, supports the yuan.

The dollar index , which measures the currency against a basket of six major peers, slipped 0.029% at 103.56, hovering near a 4-month low.

Meanwhile, higher bond yields underpinned the yuan.

China's 10-year bond yield has risen more than 20 basis points since February, as investors lowered their expectations for an imminent rate cut and funding conditions in the interbank market remained tight.

"However, one should be cautious in extrapolating the near-term trend, as the fundamentals remain weak. The trend of stronger yuan and higher bond yields will be more sustainable if domestic demand picks up," Hu said.

The spot yuan opened at 7.2350 per dollar and was last trading at 7.233 as of 0323 GMT, 53 pips firmer than the previous late session close, and 0.84% weaker than the midpoint.

U.S. President Donald Trump on Wednesday threatened further tariffs on European Union goods. This move comes as major U.S. trading partners announced they would retaliate against trade barriers already erected by him.

Trade tensions are rising and the outlook for U.S. exceptionalism could be fading, said Chang Wei Liang, currency and credit strategist at DBS, referring to the outperformance of the U.S. economy and assets in recent years.

"Even a softer February consumer price index (CPI) print could only give a muted boost to U.S. stocks," he said.

Prior to the market opening, the People's Bank of China set the midpoint rate , around which the yuan is allowed to trade in a 2% band, at 7.1728 per dollar, 711 pips firmer than a Reuters' estimate.

The offshore yuan traded at 7.2335 yuan per dollar , up about 0.11% in Asian trade.
